message:
fix(ui): give `/` and Ctrl+F a fresh DetailFind state instead of toggling the modal

`CassMsg::PaneFilterOpened`, `CassMsg::WildcardFallbackToggled`, and
`CassMsg::QueryChanged(text)` with `text == "/"` all previously
delegated to `self.update(CassMsg::DetailFindToggled)`. The toggle
preserved whatever find state was already present — including any
highlighted matches that had been seeded when the detail modal was
first opened from a search result. The practical failure mode was that
pressing `/` inside a detail view did not clear the previously-seeded
term, so the user's fresh query had to fight the old highlights and
the match cache.

Replace the `DetailFindToggled` delegation with an explicit
"start-from-scratch" sequence at all three call sites:

    self.detail_find = Some(DetailFindState::default());
    self.detail_find_matches_cache.borrow_mut().clear();
    self.input_mode = InputMode::DetailFind;
    return ftui::Cmd::none();

This guarantees:
- A fresh `DetailFindState` (empty term, no prior cursor position).
- The memoized matches cache is dropped so stale offsets cannot survive.
- Input mode switches to `DetailFind` so subsequent keystrokes feed the
  find box, not the outer search pane.

The regression was caught by the new TUI flow snapshots
(`tui_flows__keystroke_driven_command_palette`,
`tui_flows__search_open_find_in_detail`,
`tui_flows__search_to_detail_snippets_tab`) added in the same batch —
toggling semantics produced non-empty seeded state on entry; the new
behaviour produces the expected blank find bar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
